---
title: "FW 536 — Day 4 afternoon lab (v2 · avian / songbird) — Your Name"
author: "Your Name Here"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Day 4 afternoon lab — Bayesian modeling problem set 1

**Problem set version:** **v2 — avian / songbird theme**.
(The companion version is [v3 — marine & stock assessment](problem_set_v3.html); you choose whichever theme fits your interests.)

> **Note on topic scope.** These problems use **Bayesian methods** covered in the Day 4 afternoon lecture (priors, posteriors, conjugacy, MCMC, Nimble). The morning problems (MLE-only) are in `morning_lab_template_v2.Rmd`.

This template matches [`problem_set_v2.html`](problem_set_v2.html). Write your work below, **Knit** to HTML, and upload the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.

For setup help see the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html).

---

# Setup

```{r}
# Load packages you'll need. Add or remove as appropriate.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(nimble)
library(MCMCvis)
library(coda)
```

---

# Problem 4 — Bayesian Hermit Thrush survival

> You marked 50 adult Hermit Thrushes in spring; 38 returned the following year alive. Literature suggests adult passerine survival is around 0.55 with moderate uncertainty.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 5 — Bayesian Poisson detection rate for Cerulean Warbler

> Cerulean Warbler is a species of conservation concern. You ran 25 ten-minute point counts in mature mixed-mesophytic forest and recorded these per-count detection numbers:

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 6 — Bayesian regression: White-crowned Sparrow mass on wing length

> You captured 80 White-crowned Sparrows and recorded wing chord (mm) and mass (g). Simulate plausible data and fit a Bayesian Normal regression.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 7 — House Wren occupancy — a single-season model

> You visited 60 sites three times each in a single breeding season; you scored detection (1) or non-detection (0) of House Wrens at each visit. Let $\psi$ = occupancy probability and $p$ = per-visit detection probability conditional on occupancy.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 8 — Prior sensitivity for Northern Spotted Owl detection

> You observed 3 detections in 20 surveys. Compare four priors on $p$: Beta(1,1) (flat), Beta(1,9) (skeptical, prior mean 0.10), Beta(20, 30) (informative around 0.40), and Beta(100, 100) (very informative around 0.50).

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 9 — BCI vs HPDI for a skewed posterior on clutch size

> You fit a Poisson model to clutch sizes of Chestnut-sided Warbler nests, $n = 12$ nests, $\sum y = 38$, prior Gamma(2,1). The posterior on the mean clutch size $\lambda$ is Gamma($2 + 38, 1 + 12$) = Gamma(40, 13).

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 10 — Identifiability and label switching in mixture models

> You fit the Hummingbird arrival-time mixture from Problem 3 in Nimble instead of with mle(). With 3 chains and no constraint on the order of $\mu_1$ and $\mu_2$, your trace plots for the parameter "$\mu_1$" jump between two values across iterations and between chains.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# (Optional) Embed a photo of hand-written work

```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, out.width="60%", fig.cap="Hand-derivation for Problem N"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/problemN_derivation.jpg")
```

---

# Submission checklist

- [ ] Answered every afternoon problem from `problem_set_v2.html`.
- [ ] Knitted to HTML without errors.
- [ ] Photos (if any) embedded with `self_contained: true` in the YAML.
- [ ] Uploaded the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.
